ops.title: cookiesync auth cold-key path dies (ioreg SIGKILL) from headless/agent contexts
ops.premise: Premise: cookiesync's cold-key (biometric key-release) path cannot complete from any process without a GUI login session — its ioreg step is SIGKILLed on a ~5s deadline — so every headless agent (Claude background lanes, cron, ssh) fails 'cookiesync auth' and cold 'cookies'. Falsifiable: run cookiesync auth from a sessionless context with a warm daemon and an excluded peer; if it succeeds, the premise is wrong.

ops.text: 2026-07-19 portal-lane evidence: 'cookiesync auth' → 'run ioreg: signal: killed' at 56s (union path, flapping peer yasyf-home); '--browser arc' (peer excluded) → same kill at ~5s; repeated with daemon fully idle (0% CPU) → same kill at 4.5s. Load/peer hypotheses disproven.

Isolation: 'ioreg -a -l' runs fine standalone (1.2-3.6s) and as a grandchild bash -c (1.7s); cookiesync requestor/self/doctor all exit 0; ONLY the auth/cold-key-decrypt path dies. doctor 7/8 OK, the FAIL is the key-cache probe timing out.

Warm path unaffected: once the SE key is cached in the daemon, 'cookiesync cookies' decrypts with no ioreg — warming from a real GUI terminal (--ttl 30m) unblocks agents for the TTL. Secondary suspect: the Track A cookiesync→authkit 0.2.0 auth migration (2026-07-18) may have introduced a too-short deadline on the ioreg step in headless contexts.
